items.title: Semi melt-up: AMD at 50x fwd vs Nvidia 24x, Lam at 60x vs 20x decade-average
items.idea: The MS portfolio is up 78% YTD. AMD trades at ~330% TTM gains and 50x forward earnings vs Nvidia at 24x. ASML at 52x trailing vs 38x end-2024. Lam Research at 60x vs 10-year average of 20x. Applied Materials at 42x vs 10-year average of 18x. SK Hynix +248% YTD, Micron +226%. Long Semis is the most crowded trade. History warns: by the time estimate cuts arrive, IT stocks have already fallen an average of 30%.
items.tweetA: Lam Research at 60x earnings. Ten-year average: 20x. ASML at 52x vs 38x just six months ago. These are cyclical equipment vendors being priced as if the cycle no longer exists. When the estimate cuts come — and they always come — IT stocks have already fallen 30% on average before the data shows up. $LRCX $ASML $AMAT
items.tweetB: AMD at 50x forward earnings. Nvidia at 24x. The market is paying a premium to own the thing Jensen is building with. Applied Optoelectronics +335% YTD. Every crowded trade in history ended with the crowded position being right about the fundamentals and wrong about the entry. We are in that zone.

items.title: Google raised $80B from stock sales to fund AI — theoretically no upper limit
items.idea: Google raised $80B from stock sales specifically to fund its AI buildout. The newsletter frames this as removing any theoretical upper limit on capital that can be pumped into the AI ecosystem. Until now, investors expected peak demand; that assumption has been shattered.
items.tweetA: Google just raised $80B selling its own shares to fund AI. When operating cash flow is not enough, you sell equity. This is not a mature capital allocation decision — this is an arms race decision. That $80B shows up in Nvidia, Marvell, Credo, and power equipment earnings within 18 months. $GOOGL $NVDA
items.tweetB: The hyperscalers have stopped talking about AI ROI and started talking about theoretically no upper limit on what they can invest. Google raised $80B from its own shares. This is how bubbles and generational revolutions look identical from the inside.

items.title: Gold replaces US Treasuries as world's top reserve asset — ECB data confirms structural shift
items.idea: Per the ECB annual report: gold now accounts for 27% of all global central bank reserve assets (up from 20% a year earlier). US Treasuries fell to 22% from 25%. Dollar-denominated assets broadly at 42%, down from ~60% 25 years ago.
items.tweetA: Gold: 27% of global central bank reserve assets. US Treasuries: 22%. Dollar total: 42%, down from 60% 25 years ago. The ECB just published the data that explains why gold is at $4,450. Central banks don't chase momentum — they respond to fiduciary incentives. This shift is structural. $GLD $GC_F
items.tweetB: For the first time in modern history, gold has overtaken US Treasuries as the world's top reserve asset. The ECB framed it plainly. This is not a geopolitical statement — it is 150+ central bank finance committees independently reaching the same conclusion about where to store sovereign wealth.

items.title: BoJ Governor Ueda signals rate hike at upcoming meeting — upside inflation risks appearing sooner
items.idea: BoJ Governor Ueda, speaking for the last time before the upcoming meeting, said the Bank will continue to raise rates and upside price risks are appearing sooner. JGB yields rose, yen little changed — the market is skeptical until delivery.
items.tweetA: BoJ Governor Ueda this morning, speaking before the next meeting: I think the Bank will continue to raise the policy interest rate at an appropriate pace. Upside inflation risks are appearing sooner. JGBs sold off. The yen barely moved. That skepticism is the setup for a sharp $USDJPY move. $FXY
items.tweetB: Every BoJ hike surprises markets because markets price the probability wrong until it's done. Ueda gave as clear forward guidance today as he has all year. The yen carry trade is enormous. The unwind is not priced. Watch what happens to USDJPY when this delivers.

items.title: LMI Transportation Prices hit all-time record 96 — Hormuz driving unprecedented logistics shock
items.idea: From the May Logistics Managers Index: Transportation Prices rose to 96, the fastest rate of expansion ever recorded in the nearly ten-year history of the index. Transportation Capacity contracting at 31.7. Aggregate logistics costs at 250.9, highest since March 2022. The LMI attributes this to the Strait of Hormuz closure.
items.tweetA: LMI Transportation Prices: 96 — the highest reading ever recorded in nearly 10 years of this index. Capacity contracting at 31.7. Aggregate logistics costs highest since March 2022. The Strait of Hormuz closure is already in the supply chain. Not a risk. A fact. $UPS $XPO $FDX
items.tweetB: Everyone watches the oil price. Few watch the logistics cascade. When Transportation Capacity hits 31.7 and prices hit an all-time record 96 simultaneously, the Hormuz closure has already infected the cost structure of every company that ships anything. The inflation is baked in.

items.title: Bitcoin ETF 10-day outflow streak: $2.96B since May 15, 2026 YTD flows now negative for first time
items.idea: Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded $483.76M in outflows on Monday alone, with BlackRock's IBIT accounting for $440.29M. Cumulative ETF outflows since May 15 reached $2.96B, pushing 2026 YTD flows negative for the first time. BTC near $65,400. Galaxy Research: real directional recalibration.
items.tweetA: 10 consecutive days of Bitcoin ETF outflows. $2.96B drained since May 15. 2026 YTD flows just went NEGATIVE for the first time. BlackRock's IBIT alone accounted for $440M of yesterday's $484M outflow. Galaxy Research calls it real directional recalibration. This is institutional repositioning, not retail panic. $BTC $IBIT
items.tweetB: The product designed to permanently institutionalize Bitcoin is now the primary vehicle for institutional exits. When the 10-day outflow streak from ETFs pushes YTD negative, the Bitcoin is digital gold permanent reserve asset narrative has a credibility problem.

items.title: Strategy's existential math: $871M cash vs $1.7B annual preferred dividends, STRC below par
items.idea: Strategy's cash reserves fell to ~$871M after the $1.5B convertible debt repurchase, covering only ~6 months of $1.7B annual preferred dividend obligations. STRC fell to $96.90 — below $100 par — with 9 trading sessions until the next dividend date. When STRC trades below par, the ATM issuance mechanism breaks, eliminating the primary funding source for both BTC purchases and dividends. MSTR now ~70% below its 52-week high of $457.22.
items.tweetA: Strategy math: $871M in cash. $1.7B in annual preferred dividend obligations. That's 6 months of runway. STRC is at $96.90 — below $100 par — which means the ATM issuance mechanism that funded BTC purchases AND dividend payments has broken. The leverage on leverage is now working in reverse. $MSTR $BTC
items.tweetB: MSTR is 70% below its 52-week high of $457.22. The market treated it as leveraged Bitcoin. Now Bitcoin falls on news of Saylor's first sale in years, which forces more BTC sales to fund dividends, which depresses BTC further. This is not a narrative shift. This is a mechanical unwinding.

items.title: BVIV fear gauge +20% to 46.45 — biggest single-day jump since February 5 crash
items.idea: The BVIV surged nearly 20% to 46.45 in a single day — biggest jump since the February 5, 2026 crash. DeFi TVL sank to $78B, lowest since October 2024.
items.tweetA: BVIV — Bitcoin's fear gauge — just surged 20% to 46.45. Biggest single-day jump since the February 5 crash. DeFi TVL at $78B, lowest since October 2024. The options market is pricing a scenario the spot market is still ignoring. Vol leads price. $BTC $BVIV
items.tweetB: Every major crypto drawdown starts with implied vol repricing before the spot move completes. At 46.45, BVIV is not screaming capitulation bottom — it's screaming that the options market thinks the spot move has more to go. The question is whether this is prologue or peak fear.

items.title: OFAC sanctions Iran's 4 largest crypto exchanges: Nobitex (11M users, 50%+ of Iranian crypto inflows)
items.idea: US Treasury OFAC sanctioned Nobitex (Iran's largest exchange, ~11M users), Wallex, Bitpin, and Ramzinex under the Economic Fury campaign. Nobitex processed over 50% of all Iranian digital asset inflows in 2025. Named executives include relatives of Iran's supreme leader. Total US seizures of Iranian crypto now ~$1B.
items.tweetA: US Treasury just sanctioned Iran's four largest crypto exchanges — including Nobitex (11M users, processed 50%+ of Iranian crypto inflows in 2025). Named executives include relatives of Iran's supreme leader. $1B in Iranian crypto seized since this campaign started. Financial warfare at full operational intensity. $USDT $COIN
items.tweetB: Crypto is not censorship resistant when the government sanctions the exchange AND the executives AND the relatives of the head of state simultaneously. Nobitex served 11 million people. After today it serves zero. The infrastructure is never neutral — it's only as neutral as the jurisdiction that allows it to operate.

items.title: Galaxy Digital launches institutional OTC prediction markets — $10M Arca trade on CLARITY Act
items.idea: Galaxy Digital launched institutional OTC prediction markets, opening with a $10M Arca trade on CLARITY Act passage outcomes. Galaxy acts as principal counterparty, warehousing risk and pairing prediction-market positions with hedges across asset classes. Gives hedge funds and family offices access to Kalshi/Polymarket liquidity at institutional size.
items.tweetA: Galaxy just brokered a $10M institutional OTC trade on whether the CLARITY Act passes. They warehouse the risk and pair it with equities/commodities hedges. The Robinhood moment for event-driven investing has arrived. Political risk is now a tradeable macro asset class. $COIN
items.tweetB: Kalshi and Polymarket democratized prediction markets for retail. Galaxy just built the institutional pipe at 9-figure size. The next step: prediction market positions showing up in hedge fund allocations as macro hedges on regulatory and geopolitical outcomes. That changes what those markets price.

items.title: Credo Zero-Flap: 20-30% GPU productivity lost to link flaps; 3-digit ASPs vs 2-digit; $100M+ FY27 guidance
items.idea: Credo CEO said one hyperscaler lost 20-30% of productivity to optical link flaps before Zero-Flap. The product achieves reliability via silicon photonics (fewer lasers), PILOT software (pre-emptive failure detection), and DSP-to-PIC integration. Commercially: 3-digit ASPs vs 2-digit for standalone components — ~10x revenue per link. Zero-Flap guided above $100M in FY27 as one of three optical legs each above $100M for total optical revenue above $600M.
items.tweetA: Credo's CEO said publicly: one hyperscaler was losing 20-30% of productivity to optical link flaps. On a 100,000-GPU cluster, that's 30,000 GPUs sitting idle — the most expensive idle compute in history. Zero-Flap sells a solution at 3-digit ASPs vs 2-digit for components. $100M+ FY27. $CRDO
items.tweetB: Network reliability has quietly become the binding constraint on usable AI compute. The market is modeling CRDO as a copper AEC company. The product it skips is a 10x ASP optical system that caps how big a cluster can actually get. $CRDO

items.title: Marvell +32.5%: Jensen Huang calls it next trillion-dollar company, Teralynx T100 102.4 Tbps switch silicon
items.idea: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang publicly called Marvell a next trillion-dollar company at Computex, triggering a 32.5% single-day move. Marvell launched the Teralynx T100 — first 102.4 Tbps switch silicon purpose-built for AI clusters, sampling this quarter. Nvidia has a reported multibillion-dollar equity investment in Marvell.
items.tweetA: Jensen Huang called Marvell the next trillion-dollar company at Computex. MRVL +32.5% in one session. But the real story: Marvell's Teralynx T100 is the first 102.4 Tbps switch silicon purpose-built for AI clusters, sampling this quarter. The bottleneck in AI has shifted from GPUs to the fabric connecting them. $MRVL $NVDA
items.tweetB: Nvidia doesn't publicly anoint companies for fun. When Huang says Marvell is the next trillion-dollar company while Nvidia already has a multibillion-dollar equity stake in Marvell — that is a signal about the AI reference architecture. Optical interconnect and custom silicon. Not GPUs. $MRVL

items.title: HPE +19.5%: Q2 beat driven by AI servers — non-GAAP EPS $0.79 vs $0.54 expected, revenue $10.7B
items.idea: HPE reported Q2 non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 vs $0.54 expected (46% beat) with revenue of $10.7B, driven by AI servers and networking equipment. Management raised full-year FY26 EPS guidance to $3.35-$3.45. Enterprise AI spending has shifted from pilot to production deployment.
items.tweetA: HPE just printed non-GAAP EPS $0.79 vs $0.54 expected — a 46% beat. Revenue $10.7B. Stock +19%. The detail: AI servers and networking drove it, not legacy hardware. Enterprise AI has left pilot and entered production. This is not a hyperscaler story anymore. $HPE
items.tweetB: Investors keep asking when does AI capex reach enterprise? HPE answered today: it already did. $10.7B in revenue, 46% EPS beat, guidance raised. If the $10.7B server company is beating on AI demand, the adoption curve is further along than consensus thought. $HPE $DELL $SMCI

items.title: CFTC approves Kalshi Bitcoin perpetual futures — CBOE -8.4%, traditional exchange model threatened
items.idea: The CFTC approved Kalshi's BTCPERP — first US-regulated perpetual futures contract. CBOE fell 8.4% and was identified as most at risk exchange operator. The broader threat: if CFTC extends perps to equities and indices, CME and CBOE lose their regulatory moat in retail derivatives. CFRA downgraded CBOE.
items.tweetA: CFTC approved Kalshi's Bitcoin perpetual futures — first US-regulated perps. CBOE fell 8.4%, identified as most at risk. The 24/7, no-roll, leveraged perp structure that dominated offshore is now inside the US regulated system. If this extends to equities, CBOE's moat just shrank permanently. $CBOE $CME $NDAQ
items.tweetB: The offshore crypto exchanges built their moat with perpetuals: no expiry, no roll friction, 24/7 trading. Retail users never went back to expiring futures once they tried perps. The CFTC just let that structure in. The question is not whether CBOE survives — it is whether CBOE's multiple still makes sense. $CBOE

items.title: ADP May: 122k jobs as expected, information sector -9k (third month), education/health dominates
items.idea: ADP reported 122k new private-sector jobs in May. Education and health services led with +57k, trade/transportation/utilities +36k. Information sector lost 9k workers — third month of decline. Construction +8k, manufacturing +3k. Under-20-employee businesses added 49k.
items.tweetA: ADP May: 122k jobs, in-line. The detail: Information sector lost 9k workers for the third straight month. Education and health +57k. The US is creating healthcare and compliance jobs while shedding knowledge-economy jobs. That mix shift matters for productivity and AI displacement narratives.
items.tweetB: Under-20-employee businesses added 49k jobs in May ADP. Manufacturing +3k, construction +8k. Small businesses are still hiring into macro uncertainty. The bifurcation is sector-level: information down, services up. The headline masks which economy is growing.

items.title: Trump in NYPost: Hormuz blockade possible through Labor Day September 7 — a full summer of elevated fuel
items.idea: Trump told the NYPost that the US naval blockade around the Strait of Hormuz could remain through Labor Day (September 7), potentially meaning a full summer of elevated fuel prices. He said it's possible but likely to resolve sooner. First specific extended timeline publicly offered.
items.tweetA: Trump just told the NYPost: the Hormuz blockade is possible through Labor Day — September 7. That's a full summer of elevated fuel prices, peak driving season, peak holiday bookings, with 20% of global oil exports constrained. He said it's unlikely. Oil was +2% before he said it. $CL_F $USO $VLO
items.tweetB: The first politician to put a specific timeline on a Hormuz closure was the one running the blockade. Through Labor Day means 3 more months of record LMI Transportation Prices, record logistics costs, and structurally elevated inflation. The Fed can't cut into this. The yield curve is flattening for a reason.

items.title: DB Annual Default Study: US spec-grade at 4% above 20yr median 2.9%, Europe 4.6% vs 2.3% — era of ultra-low defaults over
items.idea: Deutsche Bank's 28th annual default study: US spec-grade default rates at 4%, above the 20-year median of 2.9%. Europe spec-grade at 4.6%, above the 20-year median of 2.3%. The key message: don't let aggregate data and market prices make you think dispersion is disappearing. The ultra-low default regime is behind us.
items.tweetA: Deutsche Bank's 28-year-running annual default study: US spec-grade default rates at 4%, above the 20-year median of 2.9%. Europe 4.6% vs 2.3% median. Markets at ATH. Spreads tight. But defaults are ABOVE historical medians. The ultra-low default regime is behind us. Dispersion is the word to watch. $HYG $JNK
items.tweetB: The credit market says all is fine — spreads tight, issuance healthy. The default data says: US and European spec-grade default rates are running above 20-year medians during the most euphoric equity environment in years. These two things cannot both be right for much longer.

items.title: Trump announces Section 301 tariffs on 60 countries — timed precisely to replace Section 122 tariffs expiring July 24
items.idea: USTR proposed new tariffs of 10-12.5% on 60 major trading partners via Section 301 (forced labor rationale). 10% for Canada, Mexico, EU, Taiwan, UK. 12.5% for China, India, Japan, South Korea, Brazil, Switzerland. Comment period ends July 6, hearings July 7, Section 122 tariffs expire July 24.
items.tweetA: New tariffs: 10-12.5% on 60 countries, Section 301. 10% for Canada, Mexico, EU, Taiwan, UK. 12.5% for China, India, Japan, Korea, Brazil, Switzerland. Comment period ends July 6. Section 122 tariffs expire July 24. The calendar is not a coincidence — this is a legal relay baton. The tariff wall never came down. $DXY $TLT
items.tweetB: Section 122 tariffs expire July 24. Section 301 comment period ends July 6. Hearings start July 7. The administration is engineering continuous tariff coverage. Anyone who positioned for a post-tariff world was reading the headline, not the legal calendar.

items.source: The Bond Beat / BMO / Morgan Stanley
items.title: 5s/30s flattest since May 2025 at 78.4bp; 71% probability Fed hike by December; Hammack may soon be appropriate to act
items.idea: 5s/30s yield curve at 78.4bp, flattest since May 2025. Cleveland Fed President Hammack said it may soon be appropriate for policy to act to address growing risks of persistently elevated inflation. Fed hike futures price 71% probability by December.
items.tweetA: 5s/30s at 78.4bp — flattest since May 2025. Cleveland Fed's Hammack (already a dissenter): it may soon be appropriate to act on inflation. Markets now pricing 71% probability of Fed hike by December. The bond market is converging on something the equity market isn't pricing. $TLT $TBT
items.tweetB: Everyone wants lower rates. Nobody wants the conditions that justify them. JOLTS at 2-year highs, LMI transportation prices at all-time records, Hammack calling for hikes, yield curve flattening. The Fed pivoted twice in 12 months. The third pivot, if it comes, is a hike — not a cut.

items.source: The Bond Beat / Barclays US Economics
items.title: JOLTS: Prof and business services +668k openings, concentrated in 1-9 employee businesses in the West — likely AI demand
items.idea: April JOLTS surged to 7.618M (vs 6.866M expected). Professional and business services accounted for +668k of the increase. 625k of 684k new private openings came from establishments with 1-9 employees. Most concentrated in the West. Barclays: likely AI-related demand for workers in computer systems design and IT services.
items.tweetA: Buried in JOLTS: professional and business services +668k job openings. 625k of 684k new private openings from businesses with 1-9 employees. Concentrated in the West. Barclays' read: likely AI-related demand for workers. The macro data is signaling an AI hiring wave before anyone can see it in output data. $QQQ
items.tweetB: The JOLTS report that everyone read as labor market strong actually has a specific anatomy: tiny tech-adjacent firms in the West opening jobs at record rates. This is not broad-based hiring — it is a concentrated AI-economy demand signal. The displacement story and the hiring story are happening simultaneously, in different cohorts.

items.title: JOLTS vacancies/unemployed 1.033 in April — largest two-month acceleration on record, DURING the Iran War
items.idea: JOLTS Job Openings-to-Unemployed ratio rose to 1.033 in April — first reading above 1.0 since June 2025. The 0.119 gain from February to April is the largest gain over any two-month period on record, outside the pandemic. This happened while geopolitical risk and business uncertainty were surging from the Iran conflict.
items.tweetA: JOLTS vacancies-to-unemployed: 1.033 in April, first above 1.0 since June 2025. The +0.119 gain from February to April is the largest two-month jump on record outside the pandemic. This happened DURING the Iran War. Every macro model would predict softening. The data is doing the opposite. Something is wrong with the model.
items.tweetB: I keep coming back to this number: the biggest two-month labor market acceleration on record — and it happened while oil surged, Hormuz closed, and tariffs escalated. Either the AI economy is so strong it's overwhelming macro headwinds, or there is a lag in this data that will be painful when it resolves.

items.source: The Bond Beat / The Daily Number
items.title: Risk On/Risk Off indicator hits 1.3 — highest since 2021; broad cross-asset confirmation
items.idea: The Risk On/Risk Off Indicator reached 1.3, highest since 2021, reflecting simultaneous risk-on behavior across credit, currencies, commodities, growth stocks, small caps, emerging markets, and cyclical assets.
items.tweetA: The Risk On/Risk Off Indicator just hit 1.3 — highest since 2021. Credit, currencies, commodities, growth stocks, small caps, EM, and cyclicals all confirming simultaneously. Last time this happened we were in a genuine bull market. But we also know how 2021 ended. $SPY $QQQ $IWM
items.tweetB: When risk appetite expands across every asset class at the same time, the correct response is not to fight it. But the correct response is also not to forget that 2021 was the last time this indicator was here. These conditions end when they end, not on your schedule.

items.title: Bloomberg: 27% of advanced-economy workers (120M) affected by AI — but JOLTS shows zero displacement in aggregate
items.idea: Bloomberg Economics: 27% of workers in advanced economies — 120M people — are meaningfully affected by AI. AI-driven disruption drove ~55,000 planned layoffs in 2025, about 5% of total job cuts, ranked 5th. Yet JOLTS shows vacancies at 2-year highs with more than one vacancy per unemployed worker. Goldman Sachs estimates AI could automate 25% of work hours over the next decade.
items.tweetA: Bloomberg Economics: 27% of advanced-economy workers — 120 million people — are meaningfully affected by AI. Yet April JOLTS shows the strongest labor market reading in 2 years, with record job openings in AI-adjacent services. AI is supposed to be destroying jobs. The data says the opposite. The lag question is everything.
items.tweetB: AI drove 55,000 planned layoffs in 2025 — 5% of total cuts, ranked 5th among cited reasons. Goldman says AI could automate 25% of work hours. Meanwhile JOLTS vacancies hit the highest since May 2024. One of these is wrong, or the displacement cycle takes longer than everyone thinks. History says: longer.

items.title: Barclays: Best AI value hiding in Tokyo — Japan offers diversified AI exposure with macro upside vs Korea/Taiwan
items.idea: Barclays Research: Korea and Taiwan are concentrated, high-beta AI plays. Japan offers more diversified AI exposure with macro upside from BoJ rate normalization and lower cyclical risk. Japan has AI-adjacent exposure across industrials, automation, robotics, and semiconductor equipment.
items.tweetA: Barclays: The best AI value might be hiding in Tokyo. Korea and Taiwan are concentrated, high-beta, already crowded AI plays. Japan offers diversified AI exposure — automation, robotics, materials, semicap — with a macro tailwind from BoJ rate normalization. Two tailwinds. Zero crowd. $EWJ $DXJ
items.tweetB: The reflexive AI trade is NVDA, ASML, TSMC, Samsung. The contrarian trade Barclays is flagging: Japanese equities with AI exposure, before BoJ hikes strengthen the yen. That's AI demand plus currency re-rating in one position. The best alpha is always in what the consensus hasn't reached yet.

items.title: Microsoft unveils Majorana 2 quantum chip (1000x more reliable) + 7 new AI models; IBM $10B quantum by 2029
items.idea: Microsoft unveiled the Majorana 2 quantum chip described as 1000x more reliable than existing quantum chips, plus 7 new AI models. IBM committed $10 billion to quantum computing investment by 2029. ChatGPT hit 1 billion monthly active users — fastest any product in history has reached that milestone.
items.tweetA: Microsoft unveiled a quantum chip claimed to be 1000x more reliable than current chips. IBM committed $10B to quantum by 2029. ChatGPT hit 1B monthly users — fastest in history. In the same week. Three computing paradigms developing simultaneously. The convergence timeline is shorter than investors are modeling.
items.tweetB: ChatGPT hit 1B monthly users. Facebook took 8 years. Instagram 6 years. TikTok 5 years. ChatGPT: 3.5 years. Every forecaster underestimated the adoption curve. Now Microsoft is saying quantum is 1000x more reliable than before. Ask yourself: what does the same adoption-curve error look like applied to quantum?

items.title: SpaceX targets $1.75T IPO raising $75B — Morningstar says worth less than half; chip stocks 73% above 200-day average
items.idea: SpaceX is targeting a $1.75T IPO valuation and aims to raise $75B. Morningstar publicly countered that SpaceX is worth less than half that. Chip stocks are trading 73% above their 200-day moving average.
items.tweetA: SpaceX targeting a $1.75T IPO and plans to raise $75B. Morningstar says it's worth less than half that. The $75B raise is the most revealing detail — that's not a float, that's a war chest. SpaceX believes it needs $75B to execute its roadmap. At what valuation does the public market actually clear?
items.tweetB: Chip stocks are trading 73% above their 200-day moving average. SpaceX prices at $1.75T and Majorana 2 is 1000x more reliable. The AI/tech euphoria is now priced into every corner of the market simultaneously.

items.title: Stablecoin velocity hits ATH 49.7x; Mastercard 24/7 settlement; Citi projects tokenized assets $8.2T by 2030
items.idea: Stablecoin transaction velocity hit an all-time high of 49.7x per DWF Labs. Mastercard introduced 24/7 stablecoin settlement. Citi projects tokenized assets will reach $8.2 trillion by 2030. MoneyGram launched MGUSD stablecoin on Stellar. Franklin Templeton partnered with MoonPay for 24/7 institutional stablecoin-for-MMF swaps.
items.tweetA: Stablecoin velocity: ATH 49.7x. Mastercard: 24/7 stablecoin settlement. MoneyGram: launched its own stablecoin on Stellar. Franklin Templeton + MoonPay: 24/7 institutional stablecoin-for-MMF swaps. Citi: $8.2T tokenized assets by 2030. Traditional finance did not fight stablecoins. It built on them. $USDC
items.tweetB: The thesis that stablecoins would disrupt TradFi missed the actual outcome: TradFi absorbed them. Mastercard, Franklin Templeton, MoneyGram — these are not crypto-native companies. They are global financial infrastructure building digital dollar rails. The adoption is already happening.

items.title: Foreign investors own record 19% of US equities — simultaneous de-dollarization of reserves and record equity buying
items.idea: Foreign investors now own a record 19% of US equities. This is happening simultaneously with central banks moving away from US Treasuries. You can de-dollarize sovereign reserves while buying US equities — these are distinct decisions with different risk profiles.
items.tweetA: Foreign investors now own a record 19% of US equities. Central banks are simultaneously moving out of US Treasuries. These are not the same trade — you can de-dollarize reserves while buying US stocks. But if the dollar weakens while risk appetite shifts, foreign holders face currency AND equity losses simultaneously. $SPY $DXY
items.tweetB: Record 19% foreign ownership of US stocks is the most underappreciated structural risk in the equity market. It's not the bears who cause the correction — it's the foreigners who rotate. When the dollar gets expensive AND equities look extended, the exit is crowded. And they all have the same FX exposure.

items.title: ChatGPT hits 1B monthly users — fastest product in history to reach that milestone
items.idea: ChatGPT hit 1 billion monthly active users — the fastest any product in history has reached that milestone. Facebook took 8 years, Instagram 6 years, TikTok 5 years, ChatGPT approximately 3.5 years.
items.tweetA: ChatGPT just hit 1 billion monthly users. Facebook took 8 years. Instagram 6 years. TikTok 5 years. ChatGPT: 3.5 years. Every model that predicted AI adoption timing was too slow. The product serving 1B monthly users should be generating more than Anthropic's $30B ARR. Ask what revenue 1B users implies at maturity.
items.tweetB: The adoption curve for AI is not normal. ChatGPT to 1B monthly users in 3.5 years is historically unprecedented. And yet the AI will take 20 years to impact employment crowd uses historical technology adoption rates as their baseline. The baseline is already invalidated by the data.

items.title: STH capitulation vs LTH distribution: how to read the BTC drawdown through cost basis cohorts
items.idea: Holder pain is not about price level, it is about price relative to a specific cohort's cost basis. Two pressure types: capitulation (price below STH cost basis — loud, historically a bottom signal in mature structures) vs distribution (LTH selling in profit — quiet, forms real tops). Current read: STH underwater, LTH comfortably in profit. This is STH capitulation pressure, not LTH distribution.
items.tweetA: Correct way to read the BTC drawdown: STH (recent buyers) are underwater. LTH (multi-year holders) are sitting on years of profit. This is STH capitulation — loud, visible, historically a bottom signal in mature structures. If LTH started selling in profit, that would be the top signal. They haven't. $BTC
items.tweetB: The analysts calling this a bearish breakdown are reading aggregate MVRV and SOPR. Those averages hide the most important thing: whose cost basis is underwater. STH capitulation and LTH distribution produce similar aggregate readings but opposite market implications. I'm watching LTH realized price. It hasn't broken. $BTC

items.source: David Rosenberg / Early Morning with Dave
items.title: Iran-US clashes overnight: oil back to $98; Trump announces Section 301 tariffs on 60 countries same morning
items.idea: US-Iran truce tested overnight with US forces intercepting missiles and drones aimed at Gulf neighbors. Oil +2% back to nearly $98/barrel. Trump simultaneously announced 10-12.5% tariffs on 60 countries via Section 301. Rosenberg's title: Big Balance Sheet Strains Emerge in Big Tech — suggesting hyperscaler balance sheet analysis is forthcoming.
items.tweetA: US-Iran clashes overnight: missiles intercepted over Gulf countries, oil back to $98 (+2%). Trump announces Section 301 tariffs on 60 countries in the same morning. This is not separate news — it's the same stagflation input. Higher energy + higher tariffs + tight labor = the environment the Fed cannot cut into. $CL_F $TLT
items.tweetB: The macro thesis for H2 2026 was: Iran cools, tariffs pause, Fed cuts. This morning: Iran strikes US bases, oil hits $98, Trump announces new tariffs on 60 countries. Three of three assumptions invalidated in one session. What does your portfolio look like if the thesis is wrong on all three simultaneously?

items.title: NewLimit (Brian Armstrong) closes $435M Series C — prototype drug reverses biological age of human cells
items.idea: NewLimit, the anti-aging biotech co-founded by Brian Armstrong, closed a $435M Series C backed by Founders Fund, Thrive Capital, Greenoaks, and others. Milestone: a prototype drug that reverses the biological age of human cells by restoring function they had when younger — not slowing, reversing.
items.tweetA: NewLimit — Brian Armstrong's anti-aging biotech — closed a $435M Series C backed by Founders Fund, Thrive, Greenoaks. The announcement: a prototype drug that reverses the biological age of human cells by restoring function they had when younger. Not slowing. Reversing. The clock on longevity biotech just moved.
items.tweetB: The people who built crypto infrastructure — Armstrong, Founders Fund, Thrive — are now writing $435M Series C checks for cellular age reversal. The thesis that keeps working: the technology builders don't stop at one platform. The same risk appetite that bet on crypto is now betting on reversing human aging.

items.title: Coinbase Ventures buys ENA tokens on the open market — rare signal without VC discount or lockup
items.idea: Coinbase Ventures bought Ethena's ENA token on the open market — unusual. Normally invests through venture rounds at negotiated prices. Open-market purchase demonstrates maximum conviction. Coinbase is already Ethena's primary custodian, wallet provider, and perpetuals venue. A new onchain savings product for Coinbase's 100M+ users launches next week. ENA +22% on news.
items.tweetA: Coinbase Ventures bought ENA on the open market today. They typically invest in venture rounds at negotiated discounts. Buying on the open market means paying full price with zero lockup protection — maximum conviction signal. Coinbase is already Ethena's custodian, wallet provider, and perps venue. This is vertical integration via open-market token purchase. $ENA
items.tweetB: ENA is 90% below its 2025 highs. Coinbase just bought it on the open market — not in a VC round. And next week Ethena products go live for 100M+ Coinbase users. The open-market purchase is the pre-announcement signal institutional allocators read as this is going to work at scale. $ENA $COIN

items.title: Movement Labs pivots from ETH L2 to stablecoin settlement for EM — LatAm, SE Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa
items.idea: Movement Labs pivoted from Ethereum Layer-2 to stablecoin infrastructure for emerging markets, reorienting to power cross-border payments and dollar access in LatAm, Southeast Asia, and Sub-Saharan Africa. Follows a token-dumping scandal.
items.tweetA: Movement Labs pivoted from competing with Arbitrum and Base to building stablecoin settlement infrastructure for emerging markets — LatAm, SE Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa. Following a token-dumping scandal. The bet: the global unbanked need dollar access rails more than they need another DeFi protocol.
items.tweetB: The L2 graveyard is filling. The survivors will be the ones that found a real use case that isn't faster/cheaper DeFi. Movement's pivot to EM stablecoin settlement is interesting precisely because it's not competing with Coinbase or Arbitrum — it's going where they aren't. $MOVE

items.title: The cap table that kills your Series A: option pool timing costs founders 4-8%, pro-rata blocks 20-30% of the round
items.idea: Three structural cap table mistakes kill Series A rounds: (1) SAFE conversion caps that imply unrealistic valuations at priced rounds; (2) option pool timing — pre-money creation means founders bear ALL the dilution, a 4-8% ownership difference vs post-money; (3) unconditional pro-rata rights where seed investors can collectively take 20-30% of the Series A before the lead investor commits.
items.tweetA: Most founders lose their Series A before the deck is open. The option pool timing: created pre-money vs post-money = 4-8% founder dilution difference. Seed investors with pro-rata rights can take 20-30% of your Series A before the lead investor commits. Most founders discover this during diligence. Too late.
items.tweetB: The Series A investor reads your cap table as evidence of how you negotiate. Every SAFE you signed under time pressure without modeling the conversion scenario is data. The investors know. Their lawyers know. The founder discovers it when the email says not the right fit for the fund right now. It was the cap table.
